votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq logic, including the extraction of digits, the calculation of the signed digit sum, and the filtering/counting logic.
The Python code correctly implements the digit extraction and signed digit sum logic, and accurately translates the specification into a boolean check.
The Python code correctly translates the Coq logic, including the extraction of digits, the calculation of the signed digits sum, and the filtering/counting of elements in the array.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. digits_of_Z_aux iteratively builds digits from LSB to MSB then reverses, matching the Coq recursive approach that builds MSB to LSB via appending. digits_of_Z handles the zero case and uses abs_n as fuel (which is always sufficient for positive numbers). signed_digits_sum correctly computes the sum with the first digit negated if n < 0. count_nums_spec correctly counts elements where signed_digits_sum > 0 and compares to res. The concrete behavior matches on all inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. digits_of_Z_aux builds digits from MSB to LSB (matching Coq's recursive append pattern), digits_of_Z handles the zero case and uses abs_n as fuel (which is sufficient since abs_n >= number of digits for n > 0), signed_digits_sum correctly negates only the first digit for negative numbers, and count_nums_spec counts elements with positive signed digit sum. The iterative Python implementation produces the same concrete results as the recursive Coq definitions.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. digits_of_Z_aux iteratively builds digits from LSB to MSB then reverses, matching Coq's recursive append behavior. digits_of_Z handles the zero case and uses abs_n as fuel (which is always sufficient for positive numbers). signed_digits_sum correctly computes the sum where the first digit is negated for negative inputs. count_nums_spec counts elements with positive signed_digits_sum, matching the Coq filter/length logic. The concrete behavior matches on all inputs.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: digits are computed for nonnegative n with fuel = Z.to_nat n (n if >=0 else 0), using // and % consistent with Coq for nonnegatives; signed_digits_sum uses abs and adjusts the leading digit’s sign; count_nums_spec counts cases with signed_digits_sum(x) > 0 and compares to res. Concrete behavior matches for all inputs.

Python computes digits for nonnegative inputs identically, uses abs before digit extraction, applies the same signed first-digit sum, and counts elements with sum > 0 exactly as the Coq spec, yielding the same results on all concrete inputs.

Python mirrors Coq: digits are extracted for nonnegative abs(n) with sufficient fuel, order restored by reverse, signed_digits_sum treats MSB sign as in Coq, and count checks >0 and equality to the count. Edge cases (n=0, negatives) match. Thus results coincide for all inputs.
